chunk_summary: This section discusses metabolic risks in children, focusing on mental health and medication-induced factors, emphasizing systematic monitoring and balanced counseling to mitigate risks like weight gain and glycemic abnormalities.
learning_objectives: Understand the interplay between mental health treatments and metabolic risks in children.
Learn systematic monitoring protocols for atypical antipsychotics and steroids.
Identify strategies to balance cardiometabolic urgency with eating disorder prevention.
Develop communication techniques to frame metabolic risks without stigmatizing families.
key_facts: Atypical antipsychotics and systemic steroids can cause rapid weight gain, dyslipidemia, and dysglycemia.
Baseline metabolic monitoring includes weight/BMI, BP, fasting glucose or HbA1c, and fasting lipids.
Recheck weight frequently in the first weeks and repeat metabolic labs at 12 weeks and annually.
Screen for body dissatisfaction, restrictive behaviors, binge episodes, purging, and excessive exercise in adolescents.
Avoid stigmatizing, weight-centric messaging; focus on healthy behaviors and family meals.
reference_quote: The AACAP practice parameter recommends monitoring weight/BMI, BP, fasting glucose, and fasting lipid profiles following consensus recommendations when feasible.
document_key_topics: Mental health treatments and their metabolic impact
Preventive strategies for obesity and eating disorders
